connection.offlineHint: WebSocket disconnected — using periodic polling. Reconnecting…

generate.noModelDownloaded: No model downloaded yet. Go to Models to download one.
generate.promptPlaceholder: A serene mountain lake at sunrise, ultra detailed
generate.samplerHint: FLUX and SD 3.x always use their native sampler.
generate.previewAlt: Live preview of the image being generated

reference.title: Reference image (optional)
reference.styleUnsupported: Style needs an SD 1.5 or SDXL model.

info.title: Optional: condition generation on an image — make variations of it (img2img) or borrow its style (IP-Adapter).
info.mode: Variations (img2img) repaints your image guided by the prompt. Style (IP-Adapter) keeps your prompt's composition but adopts the reference's style/subject.
info.strength: How far the result may drift from the reference. Low (~0.3) stays close to it; high (~0.8) changes more; near 1.0 almost ignores it. Actual steps ≈ steps × strength.
info.styleStrength: How strongly the reference image's style influences the result. Higher = stronger style transfer.

info.model: The downloaded model used to generate. Different models (SD 1.5, SDXL, FLUX…) have different quality, speed and VRAM needs.
info.prompt: Describe what you want to see — subject, style, lighting, composition. More specific usually works better.
info.negativePrompt: Describe what to avoid (e.g. blurry, extra fingers, watermark, text). Optional.
info.sampler: The denoising algorithm. DPM++ 2M Karras is a solid all-round default; samplers trade off speed, detail and how quickly they converge.
info.steps: Number of denoising steps. More = more detail and time, with diminishing returns. 20–40 is usually plenty.
info.guidance: Guidance scale (CFG): how strictly the image follows the prompt. Low = more creative/loose; high = closer to the prompt but can look harsh. Typically 5–9.
info.batch: How many images to generate in one run. Each uses an incrementing seed, so you get variations to pick from.
info.size: Output size in pixels. Use the model's native size for best results (512×512 for SD 1.5, 1024×1024 for SDXL). Larger costs more VRAM/time.
info.seed: Starting randomness. The same seed + settings reproduces the same image. Leave empty for a new random seed each run.
info.livePreview: Show a rough preview after each step while generating. Slightly slower; SD 1.5 / SDXL only.

generate.empty: Your generated image will appear here

snippets.saveHint: Save the current field text as a reusable snippet.
snippets.name: Snippet name

lora.emptyForFamily: No LoRAs for {family} models. Add some in Settings → Curated LoRAs.
lora.help: Blend LoRA adapters into this run. Download one, enable it, then set its weight. Only LoRAs for the selected model's family are shown.
lora.weightInfo: How strongly the LoRA is applied. 1.0 = full strength; lower blends it in more subtly, higher over-applies it.
lora.fluxQuantHint: FLUX LoRAs apply on the fp16 base loaded at NF4/int8 (the default on 16 GB) — set the precision on the Models page. GGUF FLUX bases can't take LoRAs.

quant.label: Precision
quant.hint: NF4 / int8 quantize the model on load so FLUX runs in ~16 GB — with LoRAs, unlike GGUF. Pick a lighter level if it doesn't fit; the suggested one is auto-selected.

gallery.title: Gallery
gallery.empty: No images yet. Generate one to see it here.

upscale.title: Upscale
upscale.engineLoadError: Couldn't load upscaler engines. Try reloading the page.

prompt.label: Upscaler prompt (optional)
prompt.help: Guides the diffusion upscaler toward more detail. Leave empty for a faithful enlargement.

steps.label: Steps
steps.help: Denoising steps for this run. Fewer is faster; 30–50 look near-identical to the default of 75. Defaults to the value set in Settings.

tiling.label: Tiled upscaling
tiling.help: Splits large images into tiles and stitches the result — bounds VRAM and speeds up inference. Auto only tiles when the image exceeds the tile size.

fidelity.label: Fidelity
fidelity.help: Face restoration only: high keeps the person's identity (more faithful, may stay a touch blurry), low restores more aggressively (sharper/smoother, can drift from the real face). 0.7 is a good start.

result.title: Result
result.empty: Your upscaled image will appear here.

picker.title: Choose an image from the gallery
picker.empty: No images in the gallery yet.

reframe.title: Reframe
reframe.engineLoadError: Couldn't load outpaint engines. Try reloading the page.

format.title: Target format
format.help: Change the image to a target aspect ratio, or pick Custom for an exact resolution. The strategy decides how the extra area is handled. Preset ratios don't upscale; a custom size may, to hit the exact pixels.
format.customHelp: Exact output size in pixels (64–4096). May upscale to hit it.

strategy.coverHelp: Crop to the target ratio — loses the overflowing edges.
strategy.containHelp: Fit the image and fill the rest with a blurred backdrop.
strategy.edgeHelp: Extend the border pixels to fill the new area.
strategy.outpaintHelp: Generate new content in the added area with an inpaint model. Tip: describe the background/scene, not the subject.

outpaint.needsModel: Outpainting needs an inpaint model (≈ {size} GB).
outpaint.modelHelp: The inpaint model used to generate the new area. Manage models on the Models page.
outpaint.promptHelp: Describes the scene generated in the added area. Tip: describe the background/scene, not the subject.
outpaint.negativeHelp: What to avoid in the generated area. Appended to the built-in default (editable in Settings).
outpaint.autofillHint: Auto-fill needs a gallery source — uploads carry no prompt.
outpaint.tuningHelp: Fine-tune how the generated border blends with your image. 50 % is the tuned default; higher is softer/wider, lower is tighter.
outpaint.maskFeatherHelp: Width of the gradient band where the AI blends into the kept image. Wider = smoother transition, but generation reaches further inward. Shown in the preview.
outpaint.seamFeatherHelp: How softly your pixel-exact source fades into the generated border. Wider = less visible seam. Shown in the preview.
outpaint.seedBlurHelp: Blur of the reflected border seed before generation. Higher = softer starting point. Not shown in the layout preview.

params.title: Generation parameters
params.help: Diffusion settings for the AI outpaint pass. Sampler, steps and guidance auto-fill from a gallery source's original settings; the cover/contain/edge strategies ignore these.
params.samplerHelp: Scheduler used for the outpaint denoising. Applied when the inpaint model supports it.
params.stepsHelp: Denoising steps for the composition pass that invents the new border. More = more detail, slower.
params.refineHelp: Runs a second, full-resolution inpaint over the new border to add detail on large canvases. Off by default: it is much slower per step (especially with FLUX). The source stays pixel-exact either way.
params.refineStepsHelp: Denoising steps for the hires refinement pass that restores full-resolution detail along the border.
params.guidanceHelp: How strongly the outpaint follows your prompt (CFG). Higher = closer to the prompt, less variety.
params.batchHelp: How many variants to generate, each with an incrementing seed. Pick the best afterwards.
params.seedHelp: Fix the seed to reproduce a border, or leave blank for a random one. Batch variant k uses seed + k.

position.title: Source position
position.help: Where the source sits in the frame: for outpaint/contain/edge it positions the image (the rest becomes the new area); for cover it pans which part of the crop is kept. 50 % = centered. With Source scale below 100 % both axes have room to move; otherwise only the extended axis does. The preview shows it.
position.scaleHelp: Shrink the source inside the frame (100 % = fills it). Below 100 % leaves room on all sides to place it with the position sliders — the rest becomes new area (backdrop / edge / outpaint). Not used by cover.

preview.label: Preview
preview.none: Pick a source to preview how the frame will look.
preview.hintOutpaint: Tinted border = area the AI will generate. Dashed line = seam; the glow bands show the blend widths you set below.
preview.hintFill: Tinted border = area filled from the existing image.
preview.approx: Approximate layout — the final render may differ.

result.title: Result
result.empty: Your reframed image will appear here.

inpaint.title: Inpaint
inpaint.engineLoadError: Couldn't load inpaint engines. Try reloading the page.
inpaint.modelHelp: The inpaint model used to repaint the masked area. Manage models on the Models page.
inpaint.needsModel: Inpainting needs an inpaint model (≈ {size} GB).

mask.title: Mask
mask.help: Paint over the area you want to regenerate. White = repaint, the rest is kept. The overlay previews the mask gradient, composite seam and seed blur you set below.

canvas.ariaLabel: Mask paint surface. Drag to paint, or move the brush with the arrow keys and press Space to paint the area to regenerate.
canvas.empty: Pick a source image to start painting a mask.
canvas.hint: Drag on the image to paint the mask, or focus it and use the arrow keys plus Space. Use Erase to remove parts.

brush.title: Brush
brush.help: Size and edge softness of the paint brush. These affect painting only — the mask feather below controls what the model regenerates.
brush.softnessHelp: How soft the brush edge is while painting. Higher = feathered strokes.

tuning.title: Seam blending
tuning.help: Fine-tune how the repainted area blends with your image. Higher is softer/wider, lower is tighter; the inpaint defaults keep the edit contained. Changes show live on the canvas. Note: FLUX Fill uses a crisp mask (mask gradient & seed blur mainly affect SD/SDXL) — the composite seam controls the blend there.
tuning.presets: Presets — one tap sets the three sliders below:

preset.retouchHelp: Small blemish fixes (e.g. remove a pimple) — tight edit that stays close to the original.
preset.replaceHelp: Replace or add an object — frees the model to invent new content and blends the edge softly.
preset.backgroundHelp: Change the background with a clean edge around the kept subject. Tip: paint the subject, then Invert.

tuning.maskExpandHelp: Grow the painted area outward before generating, so the edit overshoots your brush and swallows a subject's soft fringe (fur, hair) — otherwise a halo of the original can remain at the edge. Raise it if you see a coloured rim of the old content; lower it for tight retouches.
tuning.maskFeatherHelp: Width of the gradient band where the AI blends into the kept image. Wider = smoother transition, but generation reaches further in. Shown as the cyan band on the canvas.
tuning.seamFeatherHelp: How softly the regenerated area fades back into your pixel-exact source. Wider = less visible seam. Shown as the amber band on the canvas.
tuning.seedBlurHelp: Blur of the source content under the mask before generation. Higher = softer starting point, less anchored to the original. Shown as the softened interior on the canvas.

inpaint.promptHelp: Describes what to draw inside the painted area.
inpaint.negativeHelp: What to avoid in the regenerated area. Appended to the built-in default (editable in Settings).

params.title: Generation parameters
params.help: Diffusion settings for the inpaint pass.
params.samplerHelp: Scheduler used for denoising. Applied when the inpaint model supports it.
params.stepsHelp: Denoising steps for the inpaint pass. More = more detail, slower.
params.refineHelp: Runs a second, full-resolution inpaint over large masked areas to add detail. Off by default: it is much slower per step. The source stays pixel-exact either way.
params.refineStepsHelp: Denoising steps for the hires refinement pass.
params.guidanceHelp: How strongly the inpaint follows your prompt (CFG). Higher = closer to the prompt, less variety.
params.batchHelp: How many variants to generate, each with an incrementing seed. Pick the best afterwards.
params.seedHelp: Fix the seed to reproduce a result, or leave blank for a random one. Batch variant k uses seed + k.

result.title: Result
result.empty: Your inpainted image will appear here.

models.title: Model catalog
models.gatedHint: Gated — add a HuggingFace token in Settings
models.loadError: Couldn't load models. Try reloading the page.
models.fitTooltipGpu: Runs fully on the GPU (~{vram} GB of {total} GB VRAM).
models.fitTooltipOffload: Needs ~{vram} GB VRAM but the GPU has {total} GB — will run slower via CPU offloading to system RAM.
models.fitTooltipTooLarge: Needs ~{vram} GB VRAM; does not fit even with CPU offloading.
models.fitTooltipCpu: No GPU detected — runs on the CPU (slow).

settings.title: Settings
settings.hfTokenHelp: Optional. Speeds up downloads and enables gated models.
settings.civitaiTokenHelp: Optional. Needed to download LoRAs/checkpoints from civitai.com.

performance.title: Performance
performance.vaeTilingHelp: Decode the image in tiles — lower VRAM at high resolution (recommended, esp. for upscaling).
performance.vaeSlicingHelp: Decode images one at a time — saves memory on batches.
performance.attentionSlicingHelp: Compute attention in slices — saves VRAM but is slower. Off by default; enable only if a tight GPU runs out of memory.
performance.vaeOnGpuHelp: For CPU-offloaded models (e.g. FLUX): keep the VAE resident on the GPU instead of moving it CPU↔GPU each run. Costs a few hundred MB VRAM, saves the per-run move. No effect on models that already fit fully on the GPU.
performance.xformersHelp: Faster, memory-efficient attention (only if xformers is installed; otherwise ignored).
performance.torchCompileHelp: Compile the model for faster iterations. The first image after enabling compiles slowly; may be ignored if unsupported.
performance.tunableOpsHelp: AMD/ROCm only. Benchmarks GEMM kernels on first use for speed. On some ROCm/Windows builds the tuning cache is rejected each session (rocBLAS version mismatch), so it re-tunes every start — turn off if the first run is slow. No effect on NVIDIA/CPU.
performance.sdX4StepsHelp: Default denoising steps for the SD x4 diffusion upscaler (adjustable per run on the Upscale page). Fewer is faster; 30–50 look near-identical to the default of 75.
performance.note: Applies to generation and upscaling. Takes effect on the next run.

outpaint.title: Outpaint
outpaint.negativeHelp: Built-in negative prompt base for AI outpainting (fights watermarks, text and duplicated subjects). A reframe run's own negative is appended to this.

modelsCatalog.title: Curated models
modelsCatalog.description: The list of generation models the app offers. Edit these to fix a repo id or add a model when the community models change.

enginesCatalog.title: Curated engines
enginesCatalog.description: The upscale and outpaint engines the app offers. Edit these to fix a repo id or add an engine.

lorasCatalog.title: Curated LoRAs
lorasCatalog.description: The LoRA adapters the app offers on the Generate page. Edit these to fix a repo id/filename or add a LoRA. Each is scoped to one model family.

catalog.empty: No entries yet — add one or reset to defaults.
catalog.deleteConfirm: Remove this entry from the catalog?
catalog.resetConfirm: Discard your edits and restore the bundled default catalog?
catalog.duplicateSlug: A catalog entry with this slug already exists.

defaults.title: Defaults
defaults.help: Preselect a default in the model / upscaler / outpaint dropdowns. Only downloaded models are listed; "Auto" uses the first downloaded one.
defaults.loadError: Couldn't load some models or engines; the defaults list may be incomplete.

edit.title: Post Processing
edit.engineLoadError: Couldn't load edit engines. Try reloading the page.
edit.modelHelp: FLUX.1 Kontext edits your image from a written instruction. Downloaded on demand.
edit.needsModel: This model needs to be downloaded first (~{size} GB).
edit.promptPlaceholder: Describe the change, e.g. "Change the lighting to a night scene"
edit.promptHelp: Say what should change in plain language. People and composition are kept; only what you describe is changed.
edit.qualityNote: Great for restyling and relighting. "Enhance quality / remove blur" works as an instruction but is limited — for pure resolution and detail, use Upscale instead.

examples.title: Try an example
examples.night: Change the lighting to a night scene
examples.day: Make it a bright sunny day
examples.paint: Turn it into a watercolor painting

params.title: Generation parameters
params.help: Kontext is flow-matching (no sampler). Guidance around 2.5 is a good default; higher follows the instruction more strictly.
params.stepsHelp: Denoising steps. Around 28 balances quality and speed.
params.guidanceHelp: How strictly to follow the instruction. ~2.5 keeps it natural; raise it for stronger edits.
params.batchHelp: How many results to generate (each with a different seed).
params.seedHelp: Leave empty for a random seed, or set a number for a reproducible edit.

result.title: Result
result.empty: Your edited image will appear here.

preset.label: Preset
preset.help: The starting point. The photo is analyzed and only the stations that help are run; you can override any of them below. Switching presets resets manual overrides.

stations.title: Restoration stations
stations.help: Each step is optional — toggle it on/off and set its strength. Analysis + the preset pick sensible defaults; your choices here override them.

stationHelp.preprocess: How strongly to apply gray-world white balance and auto-levels. Higher = stronger colour and contrast normalisation.
stationHelp.scratch: How aggressively to detect and fill scratches and dust (classical inpaint). Higher catches fainter marks but may touch more of the image.
stationHelp.denoise: How strongly to remove grain and noise. Higher is cleaner but can smooth fine texture.
stationHelp.face: CodeFormer identity ↔ smoothness for faces. Higher keeps the original identity; lower makes smoother, more idealised faces.
stationHelp.prior_fusion: How much of the AI beautify (FLUX Kontext, structure-preserving) to blend in. Higher = stronger restore but more change; lower stays closer to the original. Off by default — the riskiest station. Face restoration runs before it.
stationHelp.colorize: Adds colour to black & white or faded photos (DDColor). Strength blends the colour over the original — lower = more muted, natural. Opt-in; needs the DDColor model downloaded.
stationHelp.tone: How strongly to apply local contrast (CLAHE) and black/white-point correction. Higher = punchier tone, without clipping.

models.title: Models
models.help: Pick which model each station uses. Auto uses the first downloaded model for that role — the recommended default.

beautify.label: Beautify instruction (optional)
beautify.help: Guides the structure-preserving AI beautify. Leave empty for a faithful default restoration prompt.

result.title: Restored
result.empty: Your restored photo will appear here.

compare.title: Compare (XYZ plot)

axes.title: Sweep axes
axes.help: Pick 1–3 parameters to vary. X becomes the columns, Y the rows, Z one sheet per value. Everything else stays at the base values below.

base.title: Base parameters
base.help: Used for every parameter that isn't being swept above.

compare.tooMany: {count} images — keep the grid to {max} or fewer (reduce the axis values).
compare.saveIndividuals: Also save each image to the gallery

result.title: Grid
result.empty: Your comparison grid will appear here.

engines.title: Upscale & outpaint models
engines.subtitle: Engines used for AI upscaling and outpainting. The curated list is edited in Settings.
